text:
@NathanpmYoung Sure, game recommendation thread, let’s gooo

Taiji: like a 2D pixel art version of The Witness, its puzzle mechanics feel almost entirely fresh, but the world has less depth than The Witness

raw.full_text:
@NathanpmYoung Sure, game recommendation thread, let’s gooo

Taiji: like a 2D pixel art version of The Witness, its puzzle mechanics feel almost entirely fresh, but the world has less depth than The Witness

text: @NathanpmYoung Ghost Trick: originally made for the DS, it’s got a sci-fi storyline (think more supernatural abilities, not spaceships), lightweight puzzles (much of it is just figuring out what you can interact with & what things do) but it’s very well written and worth playing for the story.

raw.full_text: @NathanpmYoung Ghost Trick: originally made for the DS, it’s got a sci-fi storyline (think more supernatural abilities, not spaceships), lightweight puzzles (much of it is just figuring out what you can interact with & what things do) but it’s very well written and worth playing for the story.

text: @NathanpmYoung Ace Attorney / Danganronpa: Both are murder mystery visual novel series, see which vibe resonates with you. Be ready they’re very text heavy. The plot in both unfolds through a cadence of investigating in the world and then debating in a courtroom setting.

raw.full_text: @NathanpmYoung Ace Attorney / Danganronpa: Both are murder mystery visual novel series, see which vibe resonates with you. Be ready they’re very text heavy. The plot in both unfolds through a cadence of investigating in the world and then debating in a courtroom setting.

text: @NathanpmYoung Outer Wilds: I adore this one, it’s a space exploration game in a cute tiny solar system, where you have to travel around and figure out the physical laws of the universe to make progress. Very Witness-like how it introduces laws and expects you to figure out the interactions.

raw.full_text: @NathanpmYoung Outer Wilds: I adore this one, it’s a space exploration game in a cute tiny solar system, where you have to travel around and figure out the physical laws of the universe to make progress. Very Witness-like how it introduces laws and expects you to figure out the interactions.

text: @NathanpmYoung Chants of Sennaar: The same but for linguistics. The NPCs all speak a made up language that you have to decypher from context. Solid game although less focused than Outer Wilds and quite a bit simpler.

raw.full_text: @NathanpmYoung Chants of Sennaar: The same but for linguistics. The NPCs all speak a made up language that you have to decypher from context. Solid game although less focused than Outer Wilds and quite a bit simpler.

text: @NathanpmYoung Undertale / Everhood: both feel like old SNES era RPGs with cute/witty dialogue and fun battle mechanics (one is bullet hell based, the other is like a rhythm game). Both games turn out to be much deeper than they seem at first sight.

raw.full_text: @NathanpmYoung Undertale / Everhood: both feel like old SNES era RPGs with cute/witty dialogue and fun battle mechanics (one is bullet hell based, the other is like a rhythm game). Both games turn out to be much deeper than they seem at first sight.

text:
@NathanpmYoung I’ll stop here to not be overwhelming but I have dozens more recommendations if I were to follow each of these threads further :D so lmk which you liked most

My joke title at my previous work was Head of Video Game Recommendations

text:
the soccer world cup could actually be great to watch if it wasn't so dense, thankfully a couple easy changes would fix it

- allow 5 timeouts for each team
- instead of 2x45m, split the game into 9x10m
- pause the game when possession changes so the teams have some time to reset
